title: The Most Creative Posts From Pittsburgh Small Businesses
subtitle: Judged by Claude on visual concept, caption craft, format fit, and surprise. Not ranked by likes.
angle: the IG posts that took risks, made craft choices, or said something specific this quarter, ranked by editorial judgment instead of follower count.
intro:
Every quarter, Pittsburgh's small business Instagram ecosystem produces a handful of posts worth studying. Not because they went viral, not because the accounts behind them are massive, but because someone made a real creative decision. This list rewards that. A 14,000-follower coffee shop can outrank a 500,000-follower chain if the post is sharper, stranger, or more honest. Follower counts don't appear anywhere in our scoring. Visual concept does. Caption craft does. Surprise does.

This quarter's top posts leaned into specificity and personality. Lorelei led the pack with a retro illustration and caption details so local they practically smell like the North Side: Pleaktones on the bill, $1 dogs, the word "glizzies" used without irony. Golden Age Beer Company followed close behind, pairing a vintage neon sign illustration with caption copy that explained their Czech lager strategy like they were talking to a friend who actually cared. Delanie's Coffee in Shadyside landed a gilded boot mug in the frame and somehow made honky tonk feel right at home in Shadyside.

Thirty posts, 17 businesses. Issue 02 starts fresh in July.

scores.why: Gilded boot mugs are visually distinctive and playful, the caption feels authentic and fun, and the honky tonk angle creates unexpected personality for a coffee shop.
